transcript.text: Hey, hey, you're muted, you're very muted, because I'm trying, I'm trying to figure out how to share screen on Zoom, because I don't remember, yeah, you and Joe just can't, can't seem to share a screen on Zoom.

Well, like, we are using Google Meet a lot of the time, so that's good in our STEM, but where do you find it on here?
It's just at the bottom.
Yeah, like, if I share my screen, can I share my ship?
Like, share my Zoom screen, you know?

I did.
I'm just, Oh, there we go.
go.

Hi, Jen.
Hey, Megan, how are you?
Good, how are you doing?
everyone.

I've seen worse, but.
That's nothing for me.

I know, yeah.
Yeah, feel like on Friday I like close a bunch and then.
I'm like, I feel like this is pretty magical right now.
Like, this is pretty good for me.
I'm trying to do better.
Yeah, no, I was on call.
Oh, sorry.

Noah, I want to hear what you thought of your call.

I mean, that's also important.
It was very important.

You know, these are important things.
It's all part of, it's all part of, you know, it's about balance.
I'm a whole person, so.
Exactly.
How's it going, Cade?
Y'all settled in?
Getting there.
A couple days in, getting warmed up to it all, but it's good.
Yeah.
Officially transitioned over to our team on Tuesday, but he's already like going up speed and working through the workflow.
So yeah, I don't know if you guys get a chance to meet him yet.
Katie, you were on the call last time, right?
Yeah, yeah, before you officially.
It was like day negative two.
Yeah, that's right.
She's throwing you in there.
It's great.
Awesome.
And if I remember, Mariah's away this week, is that right?
Yeah.
Yes.
Yeah.
She's actually going to be out until October 7th.
So it's just these calls.
Yeah.
She's traveling around Europe right now.
So Very well.
She needs that break.
Sweet.
Yeah.
Megan, do you want to just jump in or do you want to jump in?
Yeah.
Yeah.
If you want me to jump in and yeah, and I added a little thing to the agenda just at the end of the content area that I can talk about.
Okay.
Do you want to cover performance first or?
Yeah, I want to just dive into performance and we can talk.
Sure.
Sure.
So, yeah.
So now that we're kind of like rolling with our team, I want to be able to give you folks like some performance metrics every week.

This might change a little bit like week to week because I get to know that like your content a little bit better and like what's the best way to present to you like performance week over week or longer term.
This will kind of also, you know, sometimes look at like refreshes and how are they performing now that we've refreshed them and that kind of thing.
But as a general look, know, generally we'll have kind of a month on month look at the top here for like overall performance.
And then I usually kind of jump into the site performance overall.
So, yeah, we've had a like on the site, a continued drop in performance on the site overall the past couple of weeks, like I noticed last week and then this week.
However, CTR has improved slightly and the average position has climbed a few points.
So there's nothing really too worrying I'm seeing here yet.
I'll get into a bit more.
So down below that, you know, you do see quite a lot of volatility, like week over week and month over month for the content.
So, but of course, like when we see like two weeks of kind of like decline here, it's just kind of like a little flag for us to be like, let's keep an eye on that and see how it does.
Also, you're looking at kind of like a Monday to Sunday week here on this first chart.
So pretty obvious that on the weekend, clicks drop.
And that kind of just makes sense because, you know, fewer people are really kind of reaching out to read this kind of content when they're not sitting at their work desk or that kind of thing.
So then when I'd like to generally.
Jack, I ask a quick question.
you, do you think about this?
Because from my perspective, it makes like I've been seeing this drop in terms of performance and I've chatted with our web team and whatnot, talking a little bit about the analytics.
And it seems like it's coming because of the, like the, like I said, like volatility and how the SERP has been recently with all AI overviews and whatnot showing up.
Do you see that across other.
Oh yeah, absolutely.
I'm pretty sure it's all around, but I want to just, it's not just, it's like big time.

But what we are seeing with like content that's done well and content that's really helpful and like structured properly and, you know, like basically following SEO best practices is that we're getting back a lot of those clicks back in LLM search.
That's something that we can dive into with performance as well.

Just kind of looking at increases there, you know, how can we better structure content to kind of get picked up more in search, like which pieces of yours are getting picked up in search, like LLM search.
But yeah, not at all unusual to see like clicks dropping a lot just because of, you know, things like AI answers and LLMs, like a lot.
Oh yeah, definitely not just outreach for sure.
And, and, even so, like I, I'm not particularly concerned with like outreach type content just because it is so informational and it is so.

Like high value that I'm pretty sure that, you know, as long as it's like structured well and like contains, you know, information of value, which is obviously what you're already trying to do, that you'll be earning those clicks back.

And so you might see things like impressions dropping, but, you know, clicks increasing and that's kind of a good indication that, yeah, that hopefully we can gain some of that, those clicks back through search.
So, or through LM search.
So, yeah, so then just kind of diving in a little bit into week-on-week performance on the resource pages specifically, same kind of thing, like a decrease in impressions here.

This is just like looking at Monday to Sunday last week compared to the previous one, like last Sunday, the 14th, but like, and then really stable for clicks, like two clicks difference on the resources content.
So that's pretty wild that it's that close, but yeah, again, like not particularly concerning as we're seeing like ups and downs week over week.
If you look at the content overall, this is kind of, I think I look.
Like from the first Monday in June, there's like this weird impression spike like mid-July.
And then it's kind of like up and down every week.
But you'll see that like clicks are generally increasing, especially if you look back from what is this like July 7th.
And then like, you know, impression decrease say like from what is that like beginning of August and then kind of going back up again.
So not really anything too like massively volatile here.

But what I did want to ask you about was if there's anything we should keep in mind regarding like seasonality with the product and like anything on that end that you notice in terms of like buying times or like do you happen to notice any like, you know, pitches and valleys kind of over certain times?
Yeah, I think in terms of the increase there could have been because of our, we had our June 9th through 11th, we had our events, our Unleashed event.
Thank you
Usually you'll see a bigger spite when we have an event like that.
But overall, in terms of questions regarding the product, I can get back to you on that because I'm not 100% sure.
But that's a good point to check with the broader team.
Yeah, just kind of kind of help inform like, you know, it totally depends on the type of product and like what, you know, like when people are buying it.
But if there is any sort of like seasonality there or like, like you say, like events or different things, then we can often see like, yeah, spikes and valleys kind of surrounding that.
Yeah, then I just kind of outlined the top five performing resources pages right now, just kind of in terms of like week over week changes, increases.

This is a good thing that we can look at kind of longer term if we've refreshed pieces and then just kind of, you know, usually it's, you're not going to necessarily see results on that like the very next week.
But it can help us just kind of like keep our eye on things overall.
So those are the.
Top pages, and then, yeah, not so much on performance, but one thing I just wanted to bring up that maybe you could bring into your internal team if you have like, site backend type people.
But this is just something I caught, that caught my eye on Google Search Console the other day that is not unique to you.
This is something that I actually found on like another one of our accounts.
And then I just happened to look at yours just out of curiosity.

So what we're seeing here is if you go to Google Search Console, you can look at indexing and just, I was just curious to see how the pages were indexing, if they were indexing right away, or if you have like any like indexing issues.
And what I found was that you see here, you've got like a big spike of de-index or like not index pages kind of, what is that?
Like third week of July kind of thing.
And then like a whole bunch of pages that aren't indexed.
This actually isn't a problem in itself because
Because the pages that are not being indexed here, you don't want to be indexed, but what they are, are all like UTM parameter tags.
So what's happening here is that in your robots text, like the back end of the site, you need to block some of your UTM parameters, which are like for your tracking things on your URLs.
And like I say, like they're, they're being not indexed, which is what you want to happen.
But where it starts to affect things is like in your crawl, like Google crawl budget.
So it just means that like Google spending a lot of crawl budget, scanning all these UTM parameter URLs, when you want it to be spending that budget on like your actual valid pages.
Because maybe as this, like what we'll see is like, this is going to start to increase, right?
Like the more your traffic increases, the more your UTM is going to increase.
And then over time, this could affect, you know, like indexing of actual pages you want to have indexed.
So I just wanted to flag that for your team.
I'm not sure, like, who you would send that to or who does that, but I gave some, like, actual insights about, like, the types of YouTube partners.
Like, we've got Vidyard and Eva Boone and these things.
I don't know what they are, but I'm sure you do.
And, yeah, I just wanted to, like, yeah, point that out because it's a huge number of URLs.
Yeah, no, no, I appreciate this.
And normally with situations like this, I'll create, like, a ticket for our web dev.
That would be Andrew.
I believe he's on our Slack channel, too.
But if it's possible, if you don't mind sharing this information with me, I'll literally copy and paste it into one of our tickets to share with him, and he can actually get it done on his instance.
He owns that portion of the site.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah, no problem at all.
What I'll do is after this call, I'll just put it in, like, a little action thing in Slack, and then you can just, like, send it over to him.
Perfect.
And then, yeah, of course, if there's any questions around that that we can help with.
So then I'm happy to look into that as well.
But yeah, otherwise, kind of going back to the top here, everything is like on track for this week.
We've actually already like just this past hour, like finished the edits on this week's pieces already.
So those have been sent your way.
There's now eight currently in your pipeline for review.
And we've also staged all the five pieces that were ready to be staged.
So those are ready for your final review and publishing as well.
And yeah, everything's looking great.
I put next week's topics in the chat already.
I'm actually out of office tomorrow.
So I just wanted to make sure that I got ahead of that and sent them your way so you could see them.
And yeah, I'll pass it over to Megan because I think Megan wants to talk about this section here on content clusters.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, so I was just doing some additional like keyword research just to sort of build out the content strategy for, you know, once we're through these first these first.
For clusters that we're working on.
So this is the same doc that you, that I believe you saw during the like kickoff.
But basically, so I added, I added additional clusters, starting with number five, which is market intelligence and thought leadership.
So like, that's more so like going into like authority building and then you can sort of see like some of these like related keywords.
And so with these are, yeah, like I said, already did some keyword research around them and saw a pretty good search volume.
So I was thinking like once we're through the first kind of chunk, those like first, this first few clusters and we can kind of move on to these.
But definitely like you can take a closer look at this.
Like let me know what you think, if you have feedback.
Cause like we still have, we still have plenty of content to, to get through, but I'm just trying to sort of look ahead to, to what's, to what's next.
Awesome.
No, this is really helpful, Megan.
Thank you.
I'll definitely take a look and see, but this is looking pretty, pretty accurate with what kind of I was envisioning.
So, yeah.
Cool.
Okay, great.
Awesome.
But yeah, yeah, definitely take another look and we can either talk about it async or on the meeting next week.
But yeah, yeah, I know.
So I'm like, we're in a good place and have a good direction.
Awesome.
Thank you.
And then I did have a question.
think Kate and I chatted a little bit earlier this morning and we were just curious about the process when it comes.
to how you guys go about it internally, when it comes to staging the pieces and when it's finalized, just so that we can have an understanding of when we should be expecting them.
Just because I know you guys use Airtable.
We utilize Asana and we kind of have subtasks for each particular thing and each blog is its own little project.
And I like putting in the timelines of like when it's going to be staged, when it's going to be published, since Kate is the one that's pushing it live.
So just curious if there's like a set.
That time that you guys do it, like, let's say Tuesdays, like an incremental timeframe, or is it more of like on the go basis of who's handling it?

Yeah, I'd say it's probably more on the go just because of the, like, it depends on, so basically the flow is that, like, Timmy is working all the content, and then he gets it all done and ready and polished, and then he sends it my way for editing, and then I'll make edits on it, then sometimes it means, like, I send back to him for, like, some changes and updates, and then he may send it back to me again.
Once it's all ready to go, then we send it on to our, like, internal publisher, and he basically just has a queue of, like, different, like, you know, accounts that he's publishing for.
He's usually pretty quick, like, usually, like, the day after or, or, like, max, like, two days.

So, yeah, basically, it would mean that, like, ideally, everything would be published or staged by the end of the week, but we don't really have, like, a set day, because it kind of depends on his schedule and, like.
Cue of what he has done.
But if there's any way that we can make that easier with that in for you, then.
Yeah, no, that's helpful.
I think what could be helpful is I know on Airtable, if I'm not mistaken, you have like a, let me open that up because I'm not even sure.
There's like a little timeline bucket, if I'm not mistaken.
Let just go to the, I think it's under.
If there isn't, we can add it.
Yeah, there's just like a, what is it, delivery date one and finish date.
If there's a way to get those filled out, that would be just helpful for us to keep track.
then this way we can also make the changes on our end or on our end on Asana because our broader team, they like to see also what's coming.
And they constantly ask me as well, like, oh, we have these blogs, but when are we pushing them or what's going on?
I like giving them that kind of visibility too.
Yeah, I can also look to see, like there's got to be some integration that we can take advantage of.
Between, between Airtable and Asana.
Yeah.
I can share my screen to show you this because I believe I gave you this access.
There's another, let open this really quickly.
So this is basically our, see if you can see it.
So this is our growth editorial calendar at the moment.
And right now we have like, we can do it by list.
And then I assign it over to this email that I believe I had to share with me.
Like growthx labs.
I don't know if you're able to see it, but for example, for this one, right?
We have it planned out to go live on the 29th.
And then below here, I add these in, like you guys already created the, the SEO outline, the draft, the blog header and whatnot.
then Kate is going to work on promotional copy that we have here, the reviewing status and the pushing, pushing live situation.
So I'm just trying to make this match with what we have within Airtable.
So the team can see, but I'm trying to think that there's a better.
Way that we can work together where this isn't like two different places.
Yeah, I'll, yeah, I can definitely poke around and see if I can figure out how to get them to talk to each other.
Okay, that's a lot of Taps.
Yeah.
There's all just like a lot of going on.
It's dumb.
Call that out, Megan.
It's a lot.
It's a lot.
Making mine look pretty good right now.
know what saying?
Don't look at my Taps.
Don't look at my Taps.
I feel so seen right now.
I promise you there's a method.
Oh, I know.
know.
Yeah, can't even get it until Jen.
There's three under the bus.
Awesome.
Yeah, no, that would be amazing.
Also, like, was this something that you talked about, maybe with Ada previously, about, like, automating staging at all?
Like, is that possible in your CMS?
Yeah, we were trying to map out exactly how
I would love for us to find a way, if there is one, where we could work together in having one async type of flow.
Yeah, that totally makes sense.
Cool.
Yeah, I'll definitely look into that, and I'll report back, at least by next week.
Awesome.
Because there's got to be something.
There's too much technology for it to...
Yeah.
Can't be the first one.
Yeah, that's all I had on my end.
Megan, did you have anything else you want to comment?
No, yeah, just, yeah, no, that's it for me.
So, yeah.
How are you feeling, Cade?
Feel good.
My main question was just around sort of like timelines each week of like receiving some of these from like a reviewing point of view and a staging point of view.
So just having a general idea of that being towards the end of the week, by the end of the week, is a good start for me now.
So, but yeah, feel good.
Thank you, guys.
Yeah.
That's awesome.
And I was wondering if on the next call, if we can get some sort of visibility on the LLMs, like where we're standing there.
I know the team loves seeing that when there's a way that we can share that information.
That'd be great, just so that we can give them a little glimpse.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, absolutely.
We'll talk about that.
And I'm not sure, Megan, do you know if they already set up a scrunch for, I think you guys did go over scrunch stuff and possibly you guys watching, right?
Yeah, I believe so.
I'd already got that set.
Okay, great.
Perfect.
Okay, yeah, we'll jump into that for sure.
But yeah, that's all from me.
And so we'll just chat in Slack in the meantime.
But yeah, hope you all have a good rest of your week.
Thank you both.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Outreach and GrowthX teams to discuss content performance, strategy, and workflow improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=5.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Overall site performance shows slight decline, but CTR and average position have improved](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0)
  - [8 content pieces in review pipeline; 5 pieces staged for final review and publishing](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [New content clusters proposed for future strategy, focusing on market intelligence and thought leadership](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=843.0)
  - [Teams to explore integration between Airtable and Asana for improved workflow visibility](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1077.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance Overview

  - [Slight drop in overall site performance over past couple weeks](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=283.0)
  - [CTR improved slightly; average position climbed a few points](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [Weekend clicks typically lower due to B2B nature of content](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=319.0)
  - [AI/LLM impact on SERPs causing volatility, but well-structured content still performing](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)

### Resource Pages Performance

  - [Week-on-week comparison shows decrease in impressions, but stable clicks (only 2 clicks difference)](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=447.0)
  - [Long-term trend (since June) shows generally increasing clicks despite fluctuating impressions](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=489.0)
  - [Potential correlation between performance spikes and events (e.g., Unleashed event June 9-11)](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)

### Top Performing Resources

  - [Outlined top 5 performing resource pages week-over-week](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=574.0)
  - [Useful for tracking long-term impact of content refreshes](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=593.0)

### Technical SEO Issue

  - [Large number of non-indexed pages due to UTM parameters](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=630.0)
  - [Recommendation: Block certain UTM parameters in robots.txt to optimize crawl budget](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Hadassah to create ticket for web dev (Andrew) to address this issue](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=756.0)

### Content Production Update

  - [This week's content edits completed and sent for review](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [8 pieces currently in review pipeline](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [5 pieces staged for final review and publishing](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=805.0)
  - [Next week's topics already shared in chat](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=811.0)

### New Content Clusters Proposal

  - [Megan introduced new clusters starting with "Market Intelligence and Thought Leadership"](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=843.0)
  - [Additional keyword research conducted, showing good search volume](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=860.0)
  - [Hadassah to review and provide feedback](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=899.0)

### Workflow Improvements

  - [Discussion on aligning Airtable (GrowthX) and Asana (Outreach) workflows](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1092.0)
  - [Megan to explore integration possibilities between the two platforms](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1271.0)
  - [Goal: Create a single, asynchronous workflow for better visibility and efficiency](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1260.0)

### LLM Performance Tracking

  - [Hadassah requested visibility on LLM performance for next call](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1321.0)
  - [Team interested in seeing this data regularly](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1331.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Hadassah to create ticket for web dev to address UTM parameter indexing issue](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=756.0)
  - [Megan to investigate Airtable-Asana integration options and report back next week](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1271.0)
  - [Hadassah to review proposed new content clusters and provide feedback](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=899.0)
  - [GrowthX team to prepare LLM performance data for next call](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1336.0)
  - [Continue with current content production and review workflow](https://fathom.video/share/5v9yZR-8xfQe8ZWXVaUh2Hx2YG8sY6GB?tab=summary&timestamp=1359.0)

action_items.description: Create ticket for Andrew (web dev) re blocking UTM params in robots.txt to improve crawl budget
Send UTM param blocking details to Hadassah via Slack for web dev ticket
Research Airtable-Asana integration options for syncing content calendar
Prepare LLM performance data for next week's call
